title: Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC
summary: The four Artemis 2 astronauts arrived in Florida on March 27 for final preparations ahead of a launch scheduled as soon as April 1. This marks a key milestone in NASA's Artemis program, which aims to return humans to the Moon.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Associate Members
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) announced the addition of two new associate members, Astrolab and Zeno Power, which focus on planetary mobility and energy solutions for extreme environments.

title: ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions
summary: ESA will decide by June on Europe's contributions to NASA's Gateway lunar-orbiting station, following NASA's recent decision to halt work on the project. This decision comes as Europe reconsiders its role in U.S. lunar ambitions amid shifting Artemis program priorities.

title: India’s Bellatrix raises $20 million following overseas expansion drive
summary: India-based Bellatrix Aerospace raised $20 million to expand production of its satellite propulsion systems after securing its first major overseas commercial customer.

title: Building a hypersonic pathway to orbit with Mark Bigham
summary: The Space Minds podcast features a discussion with Mark Bigham, Vice President of Defense Programs at Longshot Space Technologies Corporation, about building a hypersonic pathway to orbit. Longshot Space Technologies is introduced as a new hardware startup in the space industry.

title: The ‘ground truth’ gap in AgTech: Why satellites alone can’t save supply chains
summary: Satellite technology has revolutionized precision agriculture and supply chain monitoring in AgTech, but 'ground truth' verification remains a challenge. Despite advancements in hardware, launch costs, and AI-driven analytics, satellites alone cannot fully address supply chain reliability.

title: Industry says proposed NASA changes to commercial space station plans create confusion
summary: NASA's proposed changes to its support for commercial space stations have caused concern and confusion among industry companies. The head of an industry organization has warned about the potential impact of these changes.

title: SBQuantum and Spire to send quantum diamond magnetometer into orbit
summary: Canadian startup SBQuantum will launch a quantum diamond magnetometer into low-Earth orbit on March 30 aboard a Spire Global satellite on a SpaceX Falcon 9 rideshare. Spire is providing the satellite platform, ground stations, and data processing for the mission.

title: ispace redesigns lunar lander, introduces lunar communications service
summary: ispace is redesigning its lunar lander and delaying its first mission by its American subsidiary, while also announcing plans for a lunar satellite constellation.

title: U.S. GSSAP satellites execute GEO handoff to monitor China’s Shijian-29 spacecraft
summary: U.S. GSSAP satellites have performed a handoff maneuver to maintain close observation of China's Shijian-29 spacecraft in geostationary orbit. This action is part of ongoing space tracking efforts to monitor foreign satellites.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at Kennedy Space Center ahead of NASA's historic launch around the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts have arrived at Kennedy Space Center in Florida ahead of their historic mission to fly around the moon, scheduled to launch in five days.

title: What time is NASA's Artemis 2 moon launch on April 1?
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission, the first crewed moon flight in over 50 years, is scheduled to launch on April 1. Live coverage details are available for the event.

title: Still waiting for Ahsoka Season 2? Make the wait easier with 20% off this Lego Star Wars Ahsoka Tano Jedi Interceptor
summary: A Star Wars-themed Lego set featuring Ahsoka Tano's Jedi Interceptor is on sale for 20% off at Walmart, offering fans a distraction while waiting for Ahsoka Season 2.

title: Can humans have babies in space? It may be harder than expected
summary: A study suggests that mammalian reproduction in space may be impossible due to sperm struggling to reach an egg in microgravity. This raises concerns about the feasibility of human reproduction in space environments.

title: Fireball sightings are surging across the US — here's what's really going on
summary: Bright meteor sightings have increased across the US and Europe, including over Ohio and Texas. The surge may be due to heightened public awareness or reporting rather than an actual increase in meteor activity.

title: Are Saturn's rings made of a lost, shattered moon? New evidence arises for the case
summary: New simulations suggest Saturn's rings may have formed from a shattered moon named Chrysalis, providing insights into the planet's tilt and the relatively young age of its rings.

title: 'For All Mankind' season 5: Release date & how to watch Apple TV's alt-history space show
summary: Apple TV's alternate-history space drama 'For All Mankind' has announced its fifth season release date and viewing options. The show explores a timeline where the space race never ended, with Mars as a key focus.

title: New portrait of the oldest-known supernova | Space photo of the day for March 27, 2026
summary: A NASA X-ray telescope captured a detailed portrait of the oldest-known supernova remnant, revealing previously unseen features of the exploded star. This image was featured as the Space Photo of the Day on March 27, 2026.

title: NASA's ambitious 'decade of Venus' exploration may bank on 1 probe: 'Not everything can move forward'
summary: NASA may only proceed with one Venus mission instead of three due to budget constraints, forcing trade-offs in its planetary science portfolio.

title: NASA is developing the '1st nuclear powered interplanetary spacecraft.' What about the Voyager probes?
summary: NASA is developing its first nuclear-powered interplanetary spacecraft, raising questions about the Voyager probes' power sources. The technology, nuclear electric propulsion, differs from radioisotope thermoelectric generators (RTGs) used by Voyager.

title: I Am Artemis: Erik Richards
summary: Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager, discusses his role in supporting NASA's Artemis mission, highlighting his career in spacecraft communication. His journey reflects a lifelong passion for spaceflight that began during the Space Shuttle Program era.

title: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
summary: NASA awarded Intuitive Machines a $180.4 million contract to deliver seven lunar payloads, including five NASA-funded science and technology experiments, as part of the CLPS initiative and Artemis program.

title: NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories
summary: NASA announced the categories for its Environment and Energy 'Blue Marble' Awards, recognizing excellence in environmental management and project execution. The awards highlight achievements in compliance, conservation, and stakeholder partnerships.

title: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
summary: NISAR, a U.S.-Indian Earth observation satellite, captured an image of Mount Rainier in Washington state on November 10, 2025. The satellite's L-band SAR instrument penetrated cloud cover to provide detailed surface data.

title: NISAR Views Mount St. Helens
summary: The U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR captured an image of Mount St. Helens on Nov. 10, 2025, demonstrating its ability to penetrate clouds using L-band SAR technology.

title: NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch
summary: NASA's technology and science demonstrations are set to launch to low Earth orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the Transporter-16 commercial rideshare mission. The demonstrations will test thermal protection systems, advance in-space communications, and deepen understanding of Earth's atmosphere.

title: NASA Names Scientists to Support Lunar South Pole Science
summary: NASA has chosen 10 scientists to assist in planning lunar surface science activities for the Artemis program, including deploying instruments and collecting Moon rocks. The selected team will help shape the scientific objectives for astronauts during lunar missions.

title: I Am Artemis: Michael Guzman
summary: Michael Guzman, the main propulsion systems engineer for NASA's Artemis II mission, discusses his passion for rocket science in an audio excerpt. His email signature features complex equations related to rocket thrust and specific impulse.

title: NextSTEP-3 E: Network Extension for User Continuity and Sustainability (NEXUS) Ka-Band Backward-Compatible Relay Broad
summary: NASA plans to release a BAA under NextSTEP-3, Appendix E, for Project NEXUS to acquire a Ka-band backward-compatible relay service as the TDRS system declines. The service will include space, ground, launch, integration, and operations components.

title: Artemis II Crew’s Suits
summary: NASA and CSA unveil the Orion Crew Survival System suits for the Artemis II crew, including Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, Mission Specialist Christina Koch, and Mission Specialist Jeremy Hansen, ahead of their upcoming test flight.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon Base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant change in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company, based in Ottobrunn, Germany, had planned the launch but faced an issue that led to the cancellation.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. Administrator Jared Isaacman and other leaders revealed the plans.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on ground infrastructure at Starbase in preparation for the upcoming Block 3 Starships, aiming to increase launch cadence. This development highlights the company's focus on scaling operations for more frequent missions.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from Baikonur Cosmodrome's repaired Site 31/6. The mission follows repairs to the launch pad after the previous Soyuz MS-28 launch.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center. The company aims to expand its space infrastructure capabilities with these initiatives.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the upcoming Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the historic lunar mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. This brings the next Starship launch one step closer to realization.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of launches is scheduled with nine launches planned, including missions by SpaceX's Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, and Isar Aerospace's Spectrum.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry event or development. No specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates key milestones and contributions to space exploration and science.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the successful recovery of the spacecraft.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is set for a January 2027 launch on an Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will introduce ECOS version 6 software on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to improve satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on the Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments. This initiative aims to support research and hands-on learning in space technology.

title: Xona Space Systems Raises $170M Series C to Scale Private GPS
summary: Xona Space Systems has raised $170 million in a Series C funding round to expand its private GPS technology, which offers stronger, more accurate, and secure PNT signals in low Earth orbit (LEO) compared to traditional GPS.

title: Jared Isaacman Hits 100 Days In Office With a Bang
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman marks 100 days in office with a bold statement about returning to the Moon. The announcement emphasizes an accelerated timeline for lunar missions.

title: With Artemis Changes, Europe is Left Holding the Bag
summary: ESA and European space primes invested heavily in components for the lunar orbiting station, but recent Artemis program changes have left them without a clear role. This shift highlights Europe's strategic misalignment with NASA's revised lunar exploration plans.

title: Arkadia Space Secures €14.5M to Develop New Biprop Engine
summary: Arkadia Space, a Spanish propulsion startup, secured €14.5 million from the European Investment Council to develop a new bipropellant engine.

title: Lawmakers Question NASA’s Shifting Vision for CLDs
summary: Lawmakers are questioning NASA's evolving vision for Commercial Low Earth Orbit Destinations (CLDs), as proposed by Administrator Jared Isaacman. The new plan has sparked confusion and debate among policymakers.

title: Directed Energy: An Alternative to Space-Based Interceptors
summary: The article discusses directed energy as an alternative to space-based interceptors (SBIs). It is presented as a potential solution in the space defense sector.

title: Commerce Unveils One-Stop Licensing Proposal For Novel Space Missions
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce introduced a one-stop licensing proposal for novel space missions to streamline regulatory authority amid ongoing debates over mission authorization frameworks. This plan aims to clarify which agencies oversee unconventional space activities that don't fit traditional categories.

title: PAVE Space Raises $40M for In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: PAVE Space has secured $40 million in funding to advance its in-space propulsion technology, which uses a storable bipropellant to simplify and reduce the cost of launch operations compared to cryogenic fuels.

title: Airbase Emerges From Stealth With $5M to Automate Spectrum Allocation
summary: Airbase has emerged from stealth mode with $5 million in funding to automate spectrum allocation, addressing the growing demand for spectrum management due to the increasing number of missions in orbit.

title: NASA Priorities Get a Major Makeover
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman has outlined a new plan to prioritize a commercially driven lunar economy and surface infrastructure. This marks a significant shift in NASA's strategic priorities.

title: Bridgit Mendler Outlines Northwood’s Vision for Rapidly Deployable Ground Infrastructure
summary: Bridgit Mendler discussed Northwood Space's efforts to address satellite ground infrastructure challenges by creating an end-to-end ground system designed for rapid deployment.

title: Interview With AvL CTO Chris Rose at SATShow Week
summary: AvL Technologies CTO Chris Rose discusses the evolution of tactical fly-away systems during an interview at SATShow Week. The conversation highlights advancements in this technology.

title: D2D, Sovereign Demand, and Mega-Valuations: Space Leaders Navigate Market Volatility
summary: Space industry leaders at SATShow expressed uncertainty about the market's outlook amid volatility. Discussions focused on D2D (Direct-to-Device) services, sovereign demand, and high valuations.

title: Interview With Qorvo Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings at SATShow Week
summary: Qorvo's Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings discussed the company's RF solutions, including phased arrays for LEO satellites, during SATShow Week. The interview was featured in Via Satellite.

title: Sovereign Satcom Networks Grapple With Data Security Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
summary: Nations are prioritizing sovereign control over their satellite communications (Satcom) networks due to rising geopolitical tensions. The challenge lies in defining and implementing what 'sovereign space' entails in a complex and evolving space infrastructure.

title: Investors Debate IPO Path, Financing Routes Needed to Grow Space Market
summary: Venture capitalists at a SATShow panel highlighted the need for improved financing routes to match the rapid advancements in the commercial space sector's capabilities. They debated the best paths for growth, including IPOs, to sustain the expanding space market.

title: Interview With Kepler Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis at SATShow Week
summary: Kepler Communications' Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis discusses the company's strategy for downlinking space-collected data to Earth during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Kepler's approach to addressing this key challenge in the space industry.

title: The Evolving Influence of IFC Driving Network Development
summary: Airlines have embraced inflight connectivity (IFC) as a standard offering for passengers over the past two decades. IFC developers are now focusing on deeper advancements to meet increasing demand.

title: Government Investment, Regulations Are the Key Trends to Watch in India, APAC Space
summary: Government investment and regulations are expected to drive the growth of the space economy in India and the Asia-Pacific region. These factors will play a crucial role as both an investor and market creator.

title: Interview With Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski at SATShow Week
summary: Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski discusses the first delivery of Comtech’s Digital Common Ground 7000 series modems and live multi-orbit demonstrations at SATShow Week.
